name: 4K Video
desc: In this session, we’ll explore the “resolution” of what we can see and relate it to what’s on the screen. We will explore image size, shape, and color “depth” in both mobile and fixed technologies. At the end of the hour we’ll have discovered just how far we need to go to present the eye with all the visual information it can use. If you’ve been wondering when the numbers will match the reality of what we can see, then this might just be an hour of answers!

name: Understanding Digital Video Formats
desc: The A/V world has moved away from analog connectivity and it’s not moving back. Digital video is your future! In addition to sourcing content in the digital domain, we must understand the variation in connectivity and performance. Add to this the demand for digital content protection and the realities of the Digital Millennium Copyright Act (DMCA), coupled with the proliferation of digital mobile devices and commercial/consumer convergence, and it all means that the ability to accommodate emerging standards in enterprise environments has never been more important. If you’re wondering how long your aging VGA connections in your conference rooms will suffice, you’ll find the answers in this presentation. If you need to know if HDMI or DisplayPort is the right connectivity to design into your next system, then this is a class you need to take. If you want to know how to accommodate legacy DVI-D and simultaneously prepare for Thunderbolt, then this is a training you won’t want to miss. If you are looking for a competitive edge, and you’re just not sure what all these new acronyms mean for your and your project. then this will be time well spent. Join us for Understanding Digital Video Formats – From DVI-D to Thunderbolt and More and step into the future with confidence!

name: Digital Video at Length
desc: The importance of dependable digital video connectivity continues to grow exponentially as a plethora of consumer and commercial high definition uncompressed digital sources change the nature of installed A/V. With analog infrastructure all but gone, the ability to deploy point-to-point connectivity solutions that support digital A/V at critical middle distances from 20 meters to 100 meters is vital. Connectivity runs of 20 meters or less are often best served in a native format. Beyond 100 meters we find that IP, fiber and AVB solutions typically provide the better results. But in the important range between 20 and 100 meters, essentially “in room” connections, the need shifts to cost-effective structured wiring to deliver the best efficiency and value. In this presentation, participants will explore the payload characteristics, connectivity topology and essential technologies realized in HD-over-UTP and HDBaseT connectivity solutions. We will also explore “active” copper and fiber interconnect options in depth. This exploration will focus on operational theory, comparative performance metrics and practical application of the technology to common AV installations.

name: HDBaseT Deep Dive
desc:
This course will look at NEXT, PSNEXT, FEXT, ELFEXT, and AXT in order to explore the effect of various classes of crosstalk on the digital video payload under consideration in an HDBaseT installation. Additional exploration will include Electromagnetic Interference, signal attenuation and Attenuation-to-Crosstalk ratio to better understand the environment in which the HDBaseT signal exists. We will compare and contrast this environment with its closest cousin – AV over IP.
Because HDBaseT includes the ability to deliver up to 100 watts of DC power, we will examine the nature of POE, including both standardized and ad-hoc systems. We will briefly review IEEE 802.3af-2003, IEEE 802.3at-2009 and IEEE 802.3bt. 
We won’t stop at copper. This presentation will also look at the emerging applications of HDBaseT solutions in a fiber channel (HDBaseO?) including a discussion of Valens Colligo optical interface.

name: Aftereffects Basics

name: Quick Start to the AV Industry
desc: AVIXA's introductory course providing a broad overview of the audiovisual industry, key technologies, career paths, and professional standards. Designed for individuals new to the AV profession or those exploring AV as a career. A foundational starting point before pursuing CTS certification or specialized technical training.

name: Essentials of AV Technology
desc: AVIXA's core foundational course covering essential audiovisual technology concepts including signal flow, display technologies, audio systems, and control infrastructure. Builds the technical vocabulary and conceptual framework needed for entry-level AV professional roles. Earns RUs toward AVIXA CTS certification.

name: Introduction to Installation
desc: AVIXA's course covering the fundamentals of AV system installation including cable management, rack building, termination techniques, and system commissioning. Provides practical foundational knowledge for technicians entering the AV installation and integration sector.

name: CC102 Cabling Essentials
desc: Harman Professional's course covering structured cabling fundamentals for AV systems including cable types, connector termination, signal integrity, and cable management best practices. Provides the technical foundation for building reliable AV infrastructure in permanent installations and live event environments.

name: Elements of Structured Connectivity for A/V Applications Explained
desc: This presentation will take participants on a guided tour of the structured cabling systems deployed to discuss contemporary A/V connectivity needs.

name: Elements of System Fabrication
desc: AVIXA's hands-on course covering AV system fabrication including rack assembly, cable fabrication, equipment mounting, and documentation standards. Builds practical skills for AV technicians working in system integration, installation, and touring production environments.

name: Elements of Setup and Verification
desc: AVIXA's course on AV system commissioning, testing, and verification including signal testing, display calibration, audio alignment, and system documentation. Covers the systematic process of verifying that an installed or deployed AV system meets its design specifications and client requirements.

name: CTS Prep
desc: AVIXA's official preparation course for the Certified Technology Specialist (CTS) examination. Reviews all domains covered in the CTS exam including AV design, installation, operation, and project management. The most direct structured preparation available for AVIXA's entry-level professional certification.

name: CTS
desc: AVIXA's Certified Technology Specialist certification — the most widely recognized entry-level professional credential in the AV industry. Demonstrates competency across AV system design, installation, operation, and troubleshooting. Held by tens of thousands of AV professionals worldwide and often required for employment at leading AV companies.

name: CTS-I
desc: AVIXA's Certified Technology Specialist — Installation credential, demonstrating advanced competency in AV system design and installation. Requires passing a rigorous exam covering system architecture, signal distribution, structured cabling, and project management. Widely recognized by employers as validation of professional-level AV integration expertise.

name: Lynda.comTraining for general AutoCAD online
desc: LinkedIn Learning (formerly Lynda.com) AutoCAD training series covering 2D drafting, technical drawing, and CAD workflow fundamentals. Applicable to AV professionals who create system drawings, rack elevations, and signal flow diagrams using AutoCAD. A practical skill for AV designers and project managers who need to produce or read technical production drawings.

name: AVSnap tutorials
desc: Similar to Microsoft Vizio - these are some guides to help train for documenting AV design

name: Autodesk Certifications
desc: Autodesk's certification program for AutoCAD and related software including AutoCAD, Revit, and Fusion 360. Relevant for AV professionals and system designers who use Autodesk software for AV system drawings, facility plans, and technical documentation in commercial AV installations.

name: Event Setup for AV Techs Online
desc: AVIXA's online course covering the setup, operation, and teardown procedures for AV equipment in live event environments. Addresses the specific workflows and best practices for event-focused AV technicians working in hotels, conference centers, and event venues.

name: Project Management for AVOnline
desc:
Tackle high-level project management principles with industry expert Brad Malone, PMP.
Through short, streaming videos and mixed media, walk step-by-step through customized
content to address your specific AV needs.

name: AV Design 2: Infrastructre Online
desc: Explore how a building's physical structure can dramatically affect AV design. This course emphasizes working with other trades, including electrical, acoustics, lighting, industry codes, mounting, HVAC, and architects. Learn critical considerations that will help you design better systems for your customers.

name: AV Design 3: Applied Design Online
desc: AVIXA's advanced applied AV system design course, covering signal flow design, system block diagrams, equipment selection, and design documentation for complex AV installations. Builds on earlier design fundamentals courses to develop complete AV system design capability.

name: XTP Systems Design Engineer (XTP-E)
desc: Extron's certification for engineers and integrators specializing in XTP matrix switching systems. Covers system design, configuration, signal management, and troubleshooting for Extron's XTP extended distance AV distribution platform. Demonstrates professional competency with Extron's enterprise matrix routing solutions.

name: DM Certified - Engineer-4k (DMC-E-4k)
desc: Crestron's certification for engineers specializing in DigitalMedia 4K AV distribution systems. Covers DM system design, programming, configuration, and troubleshooting for Crestron's DM infrastructure products. An important credential for AV integrators working with Crestron DM installations in corporate and higher education environments.

name: Apple Script Tutorial
desc: AppleScript Tutorial — macOS automation guide covering AppleScript syntax, application targeting, and automation scripting for Mac-based show control and presentation systems. Useful for AV technicians automating repetitive tasks on Mac-based event production workstations.

name: Canadian Centre of Occupational Health and Safety
desc: Canadian Centre for Occupational Health and Safety — comprehensive workplace health and safety resource covering hazard identification, ergonomics, chemical safety, and industry-specific safety standards. Relevant for AV professionals working in live event production environments with electrical and physical hazard exposure.

name: Drafty App Youtube Tutorials
desc: Drafty App YouTube tutorial series covering Drafty — a mobile drafting and AV system documentation app for creating signal flow diagrams, cable schedules, and system layouts on iPad and iPhone. Useful for AV professionals who document show systems in the field.

name: Harman Pro Webinar Series for LX, Sound, and Video
desc: Harman Professional's webinar series covering training content across lighting, sound, and video disciplines for AV professionals. Includes product-specific and application-level content from Harman's professional brands including JBL, Crown, Martin, AMX, and BSS.

name: Hazard Identification in the work enviroment
desc: IATSE International Training Trust's hazard identification course covering workplace hazard recognition, risk assessment, and safety planning for entertainment industry workers. Covers common live event production hazards including electrical, rigging, manual handling, and environmental risks.

name: Ladder Safety Training
desc: American Ladder Institute's ladder safety training covering ladder selection, inspection, setup, and safe use practices. Essential safety training for AV technicians who regularly work at height using ladders in event setup and installation environments.

name: Learn about LASERS!
desc: X-Laser's free educational resource covering laser safety, regulatory compliance, and practical operation of professional laser systems in live event environments. Covers variance applications, safety officer responsibilities, and technical requirements for deploying lasers in entertainment production.

name: NASA's Fastener Design Manual
desc: NASA's Fastener Design Manual — a comprehensive technical reference covering fastener types, materials, thread specifications, torque values, and installation practices. A valuable engineering reference for rigging and production professionals working with structural fasteners and load-bearing hardware.

name: Self Paced SketchUp
desc: SketchUp's free self-paced learning portal covering 3D modeling fundamentals, the SketchUp interface, and workflow techniques for AV professionals using SketchUp for set design, stage layouts, scenic planning, and AV system spatial documentation.

name: Soldering is Easy - How to Comic
desc: Mitch Altman's 'Soldering is Easy' instructional comic — a free, illustrated beginner's guide to soldering technique covering iron selection, solder types, joint quality, and common mistakes. An accessible and widely shared resource for AV technicians learning basic electronics soldering.

name: VectorWorks Daily Webinars
desc: Vectorworks' free daily webinar series covering Vectorworks Spotlight for entertainment design, Vectorworks Architect, and industry-specific workflows. Includes introductory and advanced topics for AV professionals and designers using Vectorworks for production planning and technical drawing.

name: Grog Animated Knots
desc: Grog's Animated Knots — the most comprehensive free online knot-tying reference, featuring step-by-step animations for over 100 knots used in rigging, rope work, and load securing. An essential reference for AV and rigging professionals who work with rope, slings, and lashing in live event production.

name: APTXL — Entertainment Electrics Training
desc: ETCP-recognized online training in entertainment electrics, portable power distribution, and DMX/networking. Taught by Richard Cadena (ETCP RT/CEE), courses cover Ohm's Law, 3-phase power, safety, and ETCP exam prep. Used by lighting and power techs preparing for ETCP certification.

name: ETCP Certification Program
desc: The Entertainment Technician Certification Program (ETCP) is an industry-wide credential for Arena Riggers, Theatre Riggers, Entertainment Electricians, and Portable Power Distribution Technicians. Certification demonstrates verified competency to employers and enhances career credibility. Administered by ESTA with exams available online and at select test centers.

name: Entertainment Electricity — Training & Resources
desc: Online training and reference site covering entertainment industry electrical fundamentals, safety, and ETCP exam preparation. Offers courses, practice quizzes, and study materials for electricians and power technicians in live event production. Developed and maintained by ETCP Recognized Trainer Richard Cadena.

name: Ohm's Law Practice
desc: Online practice exercises for mastering Ohm's Law calculations including voltage, current, resistance, and power formulas. Essential preparation for AV technicians working with power distribution, amplifier calculations, and speaker impedance. Part of the aptxl / entertainmentelectricity.com training ecosystem.

name: Power Formula Practice
desc: Practice exercises focused on AC and DC power formula calculations for live event production electricians. Covers single-phase and three-phase power, power factor, and load calculations. Critical knowledge for any AV professional responsible for power distribution on events.

name: Electrics Flash Cards
desc: Digital flash cards covering entertainment industry electrical concepts, formulas, and safety standards. Designed to help students and professionals memorize key formulas and reference values for field work and ETCP exam preparation. A quick daily study tool for power and electrics technicians.

name: rcad.me — AV & Lighting Reference
desc: Online reference and resource hub for AV and lighting professionals. Contains technical documentation, tutorials, and industry reference materials for production technicians. A community-maintained knowledge base covering a range of AV topics.

name: automatedlighting.pro — Automated Lighting Resources
desc: Online training and reference site dedicated to automated and intelligent lighting for live event production. Covers fixture types, DMX programming, console operation, and moving light troubleshooting. A focused resource for lighting technicians specializing in automated fixtures.

name: EESA Bulletins and Guidelines
desc: Technical bulletins and safety guidelines published by the Entertainment Electrical Safety Association (EESA) for live event production electricians. Covers portable power distribution safety, grounding, overcurrent protection, and best practices for entertainment venues. An important compliance and safety reference for power techs.

name: Television, Film, Live Performance and Event Electrical Guidelines
desc: Industry-standard electrical guidelines developed by ESTA/PLASA for television, film, live performance, and live event production. Covers safe working practices, equipment standards, and technical requirements for entertainment industry electrical work. A key compliance reference for production electricians and AV companies.

name: ESTA Technical Standard Documents
desc: Official technical standards and documentation published by ESTA (now PLASA) covering DMX512, RDM, sACN, rigging, and other entertainment technology disciplines. Used by manufacturers, integrators, and technicians to ensure interoperability and safety compliance. The authoritative source for entertainment technology standards.

name: Soldering is Easy — How-To Comic
desc: Free illustrated comic-book guide to soldering for beginners and professionals. Written by Mighty Ohm, it covers tools, safety, technique, and common mistakes in a fun, accessible format. A practical reference for AV techs who do any amount of cable fabrication or circuit repair work.

name: Animated Knots by Grog
desc: Comprehensive online knot reference with step-by-step animated instructions for hundreds of knots used in rigging, rope access, and stage work. Covers climbing knots, theatrical rigging knots, sailing knots, and utility knots with clear visual guides. An essential browser-based reference for riggers and stage crew working with ropes and tie-offs.

name: AV Company Search Database
desc: AV Company Search Database — a community-maintained directory of professional AV companies, integrators, and service providers searchable by location and specialty. A useful resource for AV professionals looking for employers, subcontractors, or regional production partners.

name: Document Library Downloads
desc: Document Library Downloads — a curated collection of downloadable AV industry technical documents including wiring standards, signal charts, rack unit templates, and production documentation templates. A practical reference library for working AV technicians and system designers.

name: Event Safety Alliance Standards & Guidelines
desc: Event Safety Alliance's standards and guidelines covering crowd management, production safety, emergency planning, and risk assessment for live events. Essential reading for event production professionals responsible for safety planning and compliance in live event environments.

name: Keuwlsoft's Apps for Android
desc: Keuwlsoft's Android apps for AV and audio professionals — including signal generators, analyzers, tuners, and measurement tools. A collection of practical mobile utilities for audio and AV technicians who use Android devices for on-site signal testing and system verification.

name: Wookiesoft AV App & Online Downloadable Tools
desc: Wookiesoft's AV app collection and downloadable tools for event production professionals. Includes calculators, reference tools, and productivity utilities designed for working AV technicians managing show systems, signal flows, and technical production requirements.

name: Adam Savage's Tested
desc: Adam Savage's Tested YouTube channel — featuring in-depth builds, maker projects, and technology explorations from the MythBusters co-host. Broadly inspiring content for AV and production professionals interested in fabrication, engineering problem-solving, and technical creativity.

name: Corporate AV (AVX Live! Pordcast)
desc: AVX Live! Podcast — the Corporate AV podcast covering topics relevant to corporate AV professionals including technology trends, project management, client relations, and career development in the corporate AV and live events industry.

name: Lightware Visual Engineering Academy
desc: Online learning portal from Lightware offering training on signal extension, matrix switching, and AV-over-IP solutions. Covers Lightware product families including HDMI/SDI extenders, multi-format matrices, and Dante-enabled products. Useful for AV integrators and technicians working with Lightware systems.

name: AV Math for Design Online
desc: Explores key math formulas used in AV system design including coverage angles, throw distances, decibel calculations, and signal level conversions. Builds confidence through practical examples and hands-on problems directly applicable to system design and CTS exam preparation. Earns 15 RUs toward AVIXA CTS certification.

name: AV Design 1: Environment Online
desc: Covers the environmental and human factors that affect AV system design including room acoustics, sight lines, ambient noise, and user experience considerations. Teaches students to evaluate a space from the end user's perspective before selecting technology. Earns 15 RUs toward AVIXA CTS certification.

name: AV Design 2: Infrastructure Online
desc: Explores how a building's physical infrastructure — including electrical, acoustics, HVAC, lighting, and structural systems — affects AV design decisions. Emphasizes cross-trade coordination and code compliance as critical skills for AV integrators. Earns 10 RUs toward AVIXA CTS certification.

name: Evolve Media Group — Learn Portal
desc: Learning portal from Evolve Media Group offering training content for live event production professionals covering video, audio, and production management topics. Also home to The Tech Forward Podcast which covers industry trends and career development for AV professionals. Free access to available content.

name: AV Tech Info Blog & Resources
desc: Resource blog covering AV technology, installation tips, troubleshooting guides, and industry news for working AV technicians. A community-driven knowledge hub with practical how-to content for integrators and live event professionals. Free access with no login required.

name: Hello Endless — Event Production Resources
desc: Resource hub from Hello Endless covering event production technology, tools, and industry best practices. Includes guides on event tech, AV production workflows, and links to the Event Tech Podcast. Useful for AV professionals working in the event production and corporate events space.

name: ClemcoAV Educate — AV Career Training
desc: Free AV career training resource from ClemcoAV covering professional development, business skills, and technical education for AV technicians. Includes the Career 101 AVE curriculum specifically designed for independent AV contractors and emerging professionals. A strong complement to technical training for career growth.

name: Atlas IED Academy
desc: Atlas IED's training and certification program covering commercial audio and mass notification systems, amplifier configuration, and system design. Covers IPX networked audio, paging systems, and life safety audio for AV integrators working in commercial installation environments.

name: Exertis Almo E4 AV Tour Education
desc: Exertis Almo's education program covering the latest commercial AV products, technologies, and market trends through their E4 AV Tour series. Provides dealer and integrator training on digital signage, unified communications, and commercial display solutions across multiple vendor product lines.

name: Draper AIA Continuing Education
desc: Draper's AIA-registered continuing education program for architects and AV designers covering projection screen selection, room design considerations, aspect ratios, and ambient light rejection. Earns AIA LU credit hours for licensed architects and AV system designers.

name: Kramer Academy
desc: Kramer's official training and certification program covering their AV switching, distribution, signal extension, and collaboration product lines. Includes online courses and certification tracks for AV professionals and integrators working with Kramer products in corporate, education, and live event installations.

name: Aurora Multimedia Academy
desc: Aurora Multimedia's training and education resources covering their IPX AV-over-IP, control systems, and collaboration products. Provides product knowledge and configuration training for AV integrators working with Aurora's networked AV distribution and control infrastructure.

name: ProAVCourses.com — AV Training & Academy Directory
desc: ProAVCourses.com is a dedicated platform for AV professional training — aggregating manufacturer academies, expert-led courses, and certifications in one place. Covers ProAV, networking, audio, and control systems across beginner, intermediate, and advanced levels.

name: Advanced Systems Group — Remote Control Interview Series
desc: Advanced Systems Group's YouTube channel featuring the Remote Control Interview Series — in-depth conversations with AV industry professionals covering system design, integration practices, technology trends, and real-world production challenges. Practical insights from working AV integrators and engineers.

name: A4i.tv's Rigging Videos
desc: A4i.tv's rigging video library covering entertainment rigging techniques, hardware selection, load calculations, and rigging safety for live event and theatrical production. Free video content from A4i.tv covering practical rigging knowledge for working riggers and production technicians.

name: Ashley's Book of Knots
desc: Ashley's Book of Knots by Clifford W. Ashley — the definitive knot encyclopedia featuring over 3,800 knots with illustrations and practical applications. An essential reference for riggers, stagehands, and production professionals who work with rope, cordage, and knot-dependent load securing in entertainment production.

name: Industrial Automation eAcademy
desc: Schneider Electric's Industrial Automation eAcademy — online training covering programmable logic controllers, industrial networking, motion control, and automation systems. Relevant for AV and rigging professionals working with motorized rigging, automation systems, and show control infrastructure in theatrical and live event environments.

name: PODCAST - Circuit & Gear - About scenic automation and show control
desc: Circuit & Gear Podcast by Harry Beauregard — a podcast covering scenic automation, show control, and the technical side of live entertainment production. Valuable content for AV professionals working with motorized rigging, automation systems, and integrated show control networks.

name: PODCAST - Shackles, Burlap, and Lies
desc: Shackles, Burlap, and Lies Podcast by Entertainment Rigging Services — a podcast dedicated to entertainment rigging covering hardware, safety, inspection, load calculations, and rigging practices for live event and theatrical production. An industry resource for working riggers and production professionals.

name: TAIT Towers - IQ Training Video Series
desc: TAIT Towers' iQ Training Video Series covering TAIT's powered flying and automation systems including the Navigator control platform. Free online training for operators and technicians working with TAIT automation equipment in theatrical and live event production environments.

name: User’s Guide For Lifting
desc: Crosby Group's User's Guide for Lifting — the industry-standard reference manual covering safe use of wire rope slings, shackles, hooks, rigging hardware, and load attachment points. Required reading for riggers, crane operators, and production professionals responsible for overhead lifting in live event environments.

name: Safety Webinar: Proper use of Shackles
desc: Columbus McKinnon's safety webinar on proper shackle use — covering shackle types, load ratings, pin retention, inspection criteria, and common misuse patterns. Essential safety training for entertainment riggers and production professionals who work with shackles in overhead rigging applications.

name: Online Rigging Training
desc: Sapsis Rigging's online rigging training seminars covering entertainment rigging fundamentals, hardware selection, load calculations, and rigging safety for live event and theatrical production. Taught by industry experts with extensive entertainment rigging experience. Registration and fees required.

name: Jessica "Clover" Rushing
desc: Stagehand & Rigging — YouTube channel by working rigger Jessica 'Clover' Rushing covering entertainment rigging, stagehand work, safety, and career advice for live production professionals.

name: Collaboration Systems Design Guide
desc: The Collaboration Systems Design Guide is a comprehensive resource for designing the full range of AV and IT presentation, scheduling and management technologies used in spaces from small huddle rooms to large conference rooms.

name: Logitech Collaboration Systems Design Guide
desc: The Logitech Collaboration Systems Design Guide is a comprehensive resource for designing meeting spaces that integrate with Logitech Rally Systems, MeetUp, and other accessories. The guide is designed for AV professionals that create versatile unified collaboration systems with latest web conferencing platforms.

name: Cisco Video Network Specialist
desc: The Cisco Video Network Specialist program enables traditional analog audio-visual professionals to extend their careers into the growing Video-over-IP network job role

name: CCNA Collaboration
desc: Cisco's professional certification track covering unified communications, video conferencing infrastructure, call control, and collaboration applications. Prepares AV and IT professionals to design and manage enterprise-grade video collaboration systems. Highly valued for corporate AV roles where Cisco infrastructure is common.

name: CCNP Collaboration
desc: Advanced Cisco certification covering enterprise collaboration architecture, multi-site deployment, QoS for voice and video, and integration of Cisco collaboration platforms. Designed for experienced AV/IT professionals managing complex corporate video and UC environments. One of the most recognized credentials in enterprise AV networking.

name: Introduction to IP Networking for AV Systems
desc: There are numerous advantages to using networks for AV systems, and we will develop a firm background on networking basics, as well as how the existing standards can be pulled together to form Dante.

name: CC104: Core Curriculum | Networking Essentials
desc: Baseline level of proficiency in the subject of basic networking essentials.

name: Networking Technology Online
desc: This is an interactive core knowledge course for all AV professionals. You will learn the basics of networking from an AV perspective, while gaining the tools you need to have a meaningful conversation with your IT peers. Downloadable labs also allow you to explore networking concepts on your own computer to see how they work.

name: Networking for AV Professionals - Part 1 (The Basics)
desc: With more and more audio devices communicating through Ethernet-based IP networks, IT skills have eclipsed a strong back as the primary requirement for a career in audio.

name: Networking Basics for Audio Professionals
desc: You want a career in audio? How’s your back?” has become, “How are your Ethernet networking skills?” That’s because these days, more and more audio devices rely on Ethernet-based IP networking for communication.

name: AirMedia 2.0 – Wireless Presentation Products
desc: The Crestron AirMedia 2.0 products are the fastest, easiest, most manageable, and secure wireless presentation technology. Included in this tutorial are lessons on unboxing, installing, configuring and troubleshooting Crestron AirMedia products.

name: Fusion Cloud
desc: This course is for Crestron Fusion administrators who use Crestron Fusion on a daily basis to monitor/manage rooms and devices. This course will familiarize the administrator with Crestron Fusion, and it’s features and functionality. This course will also review how to use the Crestron Fusion website to complete daily tasks involved with monitoring rooms/devices as well as how to run reports.

name: AV Streaming Design Guide
desc: The AV Streaming Design Guide is an educational resource aimed at familiarizing AV professionals with the technologies, challenges, and potential applications of AV over IP streaming.

name: Dante Certification Level 3
desc: Level 3 certification is geared towards those who have already been using Dante, are are looking to create more advanced systems.

name: Tesiralux Online
desc: Biamp's online training program covering TesiraLUX AV-over-IP system design, configuration, and troubleshooting. Covers HDBaseT and AES67 networking, system architecture, and integration with Tesira DSP platforms. Free certification-track training for AV integrators working with Biamp installed audio-video systems.

name: Individual's Youtube Channel that covers a ton of CompTIA training
desc: Professor Messer's free YouTube channel covering CompTIA A+, Network+, and Security+ certification content in structured video lessons. The networking fundamentals content is directly applicable to AV-over-IP troubleshooting, IP addressing, and understanding managed switch configurations. One of the most accessible free IT networking resources available.

name: Basic Computer Networking for Theatre
desc: Free introductory resource on basic computer networking concepts for theatrical production — covering IP addressing, subnets, switches, VLANs, and common network configurations used in show control, DMX-over-IP, and AV-over-IP systems in live performance environments.

name: Cisco CCNA
desc: Geek University's free online CCNA study resource covering IP networking fundamentals, subnetting, routing, switching, and network security. A useful free alternative for AV professionals preparing for CCNA certification or building foundational IT networking knowledge.

name: CompTia Network + Training
desc: Professor Messer's free CompTIA Network+ training course — widely regarded as one of the best free networking certification study resources available. Covers all Network+ exam objectives including IP networking, network protocols, security, and troubleshooting.

name: Art Net Networking Resources & Definitions
desc: Comprehensive networking reference covering Art-Net protocol definitions, implementation guides, and resources for AV professionals deploying Art-Net DMX-over-IP networks in live events. Covers Art-Net 4 specification, subnet and universe addressing, and integration with lighting consoles and media servers.

name: Professor Messer
desc: Professor Messer's free networking training resources — a highly regarded library of networking education content used by thousands of AV and IT professionals. Covers networking fundamentals, certification prep, and practical networking knowledge applicable to AV-over-IP and show control network design.

name: Entry point for accessing Audinate's full training portal including Dante certification courses at all three levels. Registration is free and provides access to Dante Level 1, 2, and 3 certification tracks plus supplementary resources. Essential first step for any AV professional pursuing Dante certification.
vendor: Audinate User Registration — Dante Training Portal
desc: Audinate's full training and certification registration portal for Dante audio networking education. Entry point for accessing Dante Level 1, Level 2, and Level 3 certification tracks — covering Dante network design, troubleshooting, and advanced audio networking for AV professionals.

name: Recommended Internet Speeds for Virtual Events
desc: Is 100 Mbps fast enough? Guide for streamers and remote workers hosting virtual events.

name: Speedtest.net — Internet Speed Test
desc: Browser-based internet speed test. Essential before hosting or joining a virtual event.

name: Home Lighting Tips for Zoom & Video Calls
desc: Practical lighting tips for video calls including key light placement and background lighting.

name: Lighting Tips for Video Calls (YouTube)
desc: Tutorial covering ring lights, natural light, and basic lighting setup for virtual meetings.

name: Look Better on Camera — Lighting Tutorial (YouTube)
desc: YouTube tutorial on improving on-camera appearance through lighting for virtual events and streaming.

name: Virtual Meeting Lighting Setup Guide (YouTube)
desc: Guide to professional-looking lighting for virtual meetings, webinars, and remote productions.

name: Unity Intercom — Cloud Intercom for Virtual Productions
desc: Cloud-based intercom platform for virtual and hybrid event productions enabling real-time crew communication from any location.

name: Agent IC — Clear-Com Mobile Intercom App
desc: Clear-Com's iOS/Android intercom app connecting remote crew to Clear-Com systems for virtual and hybrid productions.

name: Vari Standing Desks — Ergonomic Workspace Products
desc: Ergonomic standing desk products for home studio and remote production setups. Reduces fatigue during long virtual events.

name: External Webcam vs Built-In Laptop Camera — Guide
desc: Technical comparison of external webcams versus built-in laptop cameras for video quality in virtual event production.

name: 3 Best Background Tips for Virtual Meetings
desc: Three practical tips for selecting and setting up an effective on-camera background for virtual meetings and events.

name: How to Step Up Your Web Meeting Background
desc: Guide to improving your virtual meeting background for a more professional on-camera appearance.

name: 33 Tips to Look Professional in Virtual Meetings
desc: Comprehensive list of 33 tips for appearing professional and polished during virtual meetings, webinars, and online events.

name: Aten Technical Academy
desc: Aten's Technical Academy covering KVM switches, AV-over-IP, matrix switching, and collaboration technology. Provides product knowledge, configuration training, and certification for AV integrators and IT professionals working with Aten KVM and AV distribution infrastructure.

name: Netgear Academy
desc: Netgear's official training and certification program covering managed switches, wireless networking, and network security for business environments. Relevant for AV professionals who design and deploy Netgear network infrastructure for AV-over-IP, Dante audio networking, and enterprise AV installations.

name: ClearOne Training
desc: ClearOne's training and certification resources covering their conferencing audio, beamforming microphone arrays, and video collaboration product lines. Covers BMA CT, CONVERGE Pro 2, and UNITE cameras for AV professionals designing and integrating ClearOne systems in conference room environments.
